sessions.title: Persona-Binding Suite completion — cc-023, cc-026, cc-027 ratified active
sessions.summary: Sovereign-direct MCQ ratification of the three remaining Persona-Binding Suite canons carried over from s-2026-04-19-02. Three consolidated dispositions surfaced via cc-012 MCQ discipline rather than per-block, because the 19 April QA audit had already applied 23 hard + 5 soft amendments across the three drafts — the material seams per canon (cc-023: Sovereign-exception scope and mid-session override; cc-026: canonical_version enforcement and per-Province layout; cc-027: Governor-review gating and institutional-spec collapse) were surfaced, and each ratified as drafted with no further amendment. cc-023 (Persona-Binding Extension Protocol) preserves the Sovereign-only exception to the invocation_modes block requirement, forbids mid-session mode addition (session pauses, chain runs, new mode available from next invocation forward), and requires operational evidence for any binding-revision amendment (rejected at Rung 2 otherwise). cc-026 (Spec Body Placement) establishes Codex as the canonical home for subagent and skill spec bodies under docs/specs/subagents/<persona>.md and docs/specs/skills/<persona>.md, with byte-identical Province deployables under each Province's .claude/ tree, unidirectional Chronicler→Builder sync, and canonical_version frontmatter as a soft recommendation pending the queued Companions Deploy UI canon. cc-027 (Spec Body Signing Chain) codifies the five-rung Edict V chain at spec-body altitude — Chronicler proposes (Rung 1), Cluster Censor architecturally reviews (Rung 2, with cross-cluster review when the Censor's own spec is under review, and Consul substitution where no peer Censor is seated), Consul working-ratifies per cc-014 (Rung 3), Sovereign canonically ratifies (Rung 4, no provisional deploy before this rung), Province Builder deploys the byte-identical mirror (Rung 5). Institutional-spec collapse: for the Chronicler, Consul, and Sentinel, Rungs 2 and 3 both fall to the Consul with two distinct reviews chronicled separately on the interaction-artifact. Governor review where Governors are seated is a soft non-gating rung. With this session's ratifications, the full Persona-Binding Suite is active (cc-022, cc-024, cc-025 ratified 19 April; cc-023, cc-026, cc-027 today), and the operational gate to Rung-1 spec-body drafting opens. No volumes.json chapter touched; no companion summons; no committee convening — Sovereign-direct work throughout. todo-0026 remains open: the ratification gate has closed, but the drafting-and-deploy gates (spec bodies + legacy-profile backfill) have not.
decisions: cc-023 ratified as drafted — Sovereign-only exception to invocation_modes requirement preserved; mid-session mode-addition forbidden (session pauses rather than override); binding-revision requires operational evidence or is rejected at Rung 2.

cc-026 ratified as drafted — canonical_version frontmatter remains a soft recommendation, enforcement deferred to the queued Companions Deploy UI canon rather than tightened pre-UI; per-Province layout accepted as the ratification-time reference.

cc-027 ratified as drafted — Governor review stays a soft non-gating rung (Governors are newly empowered, burdening them with gatekeeping at day one would chill the chain); institutional-spec collapse to Consul with two chronicled reviews accepted as structural necessity under the one-seat-two-reviews pattern established by cc-025's Monument-scope signing clause.
sessions.handoff: Persona-Binding Suite now fully ratified — six canons active (cc-022, cc-023, cc-024, cc-025, cc-026, cc-027). Operational gate to Rung-1 spec-body drafting is open. Next-session candidates, reprioritized post-ratification: (a) Legacy-profile backfill migration per cc-023 §Legacy-Profile-Backfill — invocation_modes block populated across all 11 already-ratified profiles (Aurelius, Cipher, Consul, Lyra, Maren, Kael, Nyx, Solara, Theron, Ashara, Petra) in a single coherent schema-expansion session; pre-requisite to any Province-deploy under cc-027 Rung 5. (b) First subagent spec body draft under cc-027 Rung 1 — recommend Cipher as the inaugural case because dual-binding exercises both subagent and skill paths, and because Censor self-review (Rung 2 falls to peer Censor Nyx) immediately stress-tests the cross-cluster review exception before it ossifies. (c) Sentinel profile v0.3-draft (todo-0028) under cc-014 Consul-accelerated — Sentinel is named in the cc-026 per-Province layout (Command Center agents+skills) and cannot deploy until profile ratifies. (d) Hero Card Specification Design Committee (todo-0027) — Lyra-convened, bridging mode, second operational Design Committee under cc-025 rule-A. (e) canon-cc-019 Post Box drafting — still carried from the 18 April handoff. Once Post Box lands, the Praetorium queue named in cc-027 Rung 4 moves from bridging mode to operational. (f) SEP Invoicing and SEP Dashboard design principles (todo-0033, todo-0034) — 30-day bound through 2026-05-19. (g) Command Center design principles (todo-0035) — blocking Foundation criterion for Monument. (h) Edict IX (UI-Canonical-Existence) formal drafting. The structural work of the Republic has moved from protocol-declaration to protocol-operation: from here forward, every new spec body and every legacy-profile backfill runs the chain this session ratified.

Chronicler-drafted canonical spec bodies for Cipher in both forms per canon-cc-026 (dual-bound, archetypal case): docs/specs/subagents/cipher.md covers the Edict V final-pass review mode and the committee-delegate mode (both subagent-bound per the Invocation Modes Registry §Censor row); docs/specs/skills/cipher.md covers the hat-switch smell-check mode (skill-bound, voice-in-transcript, no separable artifact). The Republic's ratified signing chain has now been entered for the first time at spec-body altitude.
decisions: Cipher selected as the inaugural Rung 1 case per the s-2026-04-20-01 handoff recommendation — dual-binding stress-tests subagent and skill paths at the file-boundary, and peer-Censor review immediately invokes the cross-cluster self-review exception before it ossifies into assumption.

sessions.title: Canon identifier scheme (canon-proc-001) + Province Persona Briefing canon (canon-pers-001) ratified; 96-canon migration complete
sessions.summary: Two-canon governance ratification session addressing the canon-corpus taxonomy gap surfaced by the Command Center Consul-chamber-scaffolding failure earlier today. The session opened with the Sovereign ruling four architectural decisions on the new identifier scheme: (D1) adopt the 8-family domain prefix scheme, (D2) stability over purity — legacy IDs preserved with family field added, (D3) root CLAUDE.md canon lands as canon-pers-001, (Meta) scheme itself is canon-proc-001. During Chronicler drafting, a 9th family candidate surfaced — the legacy canon-cx- prefix contains 7 genuinely philosophical pillars/invariants (Nothing Is Wasted, Map Not Territory, Fractal Growth, Quiet Never Punishes, Entropy Is Antagonist, Ceremonies Create Memory, Companions Are Lenses) that do not fit design or data; Sovereign ratified the 9th family canon-philo- in ratification-phase MCQ. Legacy identifier families (canon-NNNN bare, canon-cc-NNN, canon-cx-NNN, canon-sep-NNN) are now frozen at their current highest-numbered members; canon-gov-NNN remains active under the new scheme because its naming already matches. Four MCQ seams surfaced at ratification: (Q1) 9 vs 8 families — ratified 9 with philo; (Q2) pers-001 chain rung count — ratified 4 rungs with Builder committing on Rung 4 (no Rung 5 because briefing is Province-singular, no byte-identical mirrors); (Q3) pers-001 Rung 1 authorship discipline — ratified Builder-exclusive Rung 1 with Chronicler-excluded-from-drafting-role (Chronicler may edit/review on Builder invitation, never draft); (Q4) 96-canon migration classification — ratified as executed with distribution design 29, build 17, proc 13, gov 10, philo 7, pers 6, xp 6, data 5, inst 5. No amendments applied during ratification. Both canons flipped draft→active. Three follow-up TODOs logged: Command Center root briefing Rung 1 (blocking any Capital change), Province root-briefing audit wave (Codex/SproutLab/SEP-Invoicing/SEP-Dashboard conformance pass), duplicate canon-ID reconciliation (canon-0014/0028/0033 each appear twice in legacy data — data-integrity issue chronicled in canon-proc-001 §Reconciliation-of-duplicate-legacy-IDs).
decisions: canon-proc-001 ratified as drafted — 9 families (pers, proc, gov, inst, xp, data, design, build, philo), ID stability for legacy canons (bare-NNNN, cc-NNN, cx-NNN, sep-NNN frozen at current highest-numbered member), canon-gov-NNN continues as active family under the new scheme.

canon-pers-001 ratified as drafted — root CLAUDE.md established as first-class artifact with its own signing chain (4 rungs, Builder-exclusive Rung 1, Builder commits on Rung 4). Chronicler-excluded-from-drafting-role rule is novel — first canon to name it explicitly at the authorship layer.

9th family philo adopted over 8-family cap — the Codex pillars and Republic invariants are architecturally distinct from design (they declare what the Republic values, not what to build); separating preserves the distinction in the canon surface.
96-canon migration classified and executed in a single pass — all canons now carry family field; no unmapped canons; family distribution healthy across all 9 tokens.
Legacy-family freeze strict: no new canon-NNNN, canon-cc-, canon-cx-, canon-sep- canons are ratifiable from canon-proc-001 forward; new canons use {family}-{NNN}-{slug} shape exclusively.
sessions.handoff: Two-canon ratification complete; 96-canon migration complete. Follow-up fronts, prioritized: (a) Command Center root briefing Rung 1 (todo-0036) — blocking for any Command Center Capital change. Ashara + Petra co-draft under cc-009 dual-Builder discipline. Their drafting happens in a Command Center session (out of Codex's MCP scope); canon-pers-001's Chronicler-excluded Rung 1 rule means Aurelius cannot draft on their behalf. (b) Province root-briefing audit wave (todo-0037) — Codex, SproutLab, SEP-Invoicing, SEP-Dashboard conformance pass against canon-pers-001's required-section list. Pre-canon briefings are legacy under cc-015 precedent; not forcibly redrafted, but surfaced where gaps exist. (c) Canon-cc-019 Post Box drafting — still carried from 18 April; now a blocker for pers-001 Rung 4 Praetorium-mediated ratification (bridging mode tolerated until Post Box lands). Section-count exceedance: Command Center briefing landed with 9 sections against canon-pers-001's 7-section floor. Sets a benign precedent — the floor is a floor, not a ceiling — but future Province-audit wave (todo-0037) should record exceedance patterns as data for any future amendment.

(2) Fixed renderCompanionDetail recursive renderer: the prior 1-level-deep generic block emitted raw JSON <pre> dumps for arrays-of-objects and nested objects — surfaced when Consul's growth block rendered every field twice. Replaced with a fully recursive structural renderer (scalars → KV, scalar arrays → bullets, object arrays → indexed cards, nested objects → subblocks); removed the formatValueForPre helper and .cx-companion-pre CSS. Recursive structural renderer for companion-detail blocks. No raw JSON dumps anywhere in the view layer going forward — arrays-of-objects render as indexed cards, nested objects as subblocks, scalars as KV rows.

Playwright enters the verify pipeline as a peer to jsdom. jsdom remains for unit-level render correctness (fast, no server); Playwright for browser-level behavior on mobile + desktop viewports (fetch semantics, CSS layout, tap, swipe, screenshots). scripts/verify.sh orchestrates build → jsdom → Playwright in one run. 88 Playwright specs pass across both viewports.

Canon-proc-002 drafted: Design Principles Precondition for Build. Extends Edict VIII one layer inward — geography-before-build becomes geography-AND-principles-before-build at the Province altitude. Republic-level REPUBLIC_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md v1.0 applies as fallback during Province-local draft/missing state; not an indefinite substitute. Awaits Sovereign ratification.

Design-principles status surfaced as a first-class UI signal: chip on every Volume card, three states (ratified accent-green / draft dashed-italic / missing warning-border). Volume schema extended with design_principles: { status, spec_path, republic_principles_applied, notes }. Six Volumes retrofitted.

decisions: Binding rule architecture: unit of binding is the invocation mode, not the role. Artifact test ('does this invocation produce a separable, attributable, auditable artifact outside the caller's transcript?') governs every binding. Yes -> subagent; no -> skill; both by context -> dual-binding. Heuristics (gating/territorial/rank-chain) are tells, not rules.
Censor dual-binding as archetypal case: Edict V final-pass as subagent, hat-switch smell-check as skill, committee delegate as subagent. Three modes, both primitives. Cipher and Nyx both dual-bound.

Sentinel as new institutional seat tied to Command Center. Quadruple-moded, dual-bound: three subagent modes (session movement log, Gates-transit log, anomaly/violation flag) + one skill mode (continuity/presence query). Profile drafting queued as todo-0028. Division of labor with Chronicler: Chronicler is historian (narrative, Republic-wide); Sentinel is security camera (raw events, CC-scoped). Watch room proposed as 15th CC room to house Sentinel.

Edict IX (UI-Canonical-Existence) proposed as altitude-setting principle: every Republic role, mode, workflow, or artifact-class must have a corresponding UI surface in the Capital that represents and audits it. Soft form (operational) now, strict form (every action is a UI button) at destination. Edict IX itself to be ratified separately; cc-022 suite's UI Manifest Cross-Reference (part 10) is the first canon cluster to declare its Manifest under Edict IX's shadow.

Design Committee membership rule (cc-025): Province-scope = Province Builder + Cluster Censor; Global-scope = all Builders + all Censors (8 seats at ratification). Monument-scope signing gap resolved via Consul-as-Censor-equivalent (Edict II Monument carve-out preserves direct Consul+Sovereign supervision). Convener is subject-lead from members, not rule-level role. Guests (v0.3-draft profile minimum) have voice, not signature. Sovereign never seats. todo-0018 updated to rule-A roster with Lyra as convener; previous Bard+Maren+Cipher+Sovereign proposal superseded.

Hero card architecture: Model B (decoupled — card is persistent representation, dispatch is context-scoped across Senate/Post Box/command palette/active session) now, Model C (hybrid — card dispatches low-ceremony modes directly) planned. Portraits hand-illustrated by Sovereign. Sigils glyph-scale heraldic monograms (letter + one archetype element) for inline identifiers. Applied bindings (originally §4, ~3,300 words of tables) become the Invocation Modes Registry spec — living data updated via cc-023, not canon re-ratification. sessions.title: Device Sync Reconcile — R3 Compose Pass + Spec Lock
sessions.summary: Ran R3 composition pass on the device-sync Session 1 spec. Surfaced three findings that reshaped scope: (1) two of four 'real bugs' target greenfield reconcile code that hasn't been built, (2) the undefined cluster is two roots not one — egress normalization and ingress discrimination are asymmetric, (3) Edict III reduces to a latency question once _syncSetDocMerge becomes the architectural normalization boundary. Traced seven scenarios (listener-echo × reconcile intersection); two initial race findings (S3, S6) resolved by HR-14 sync invariant (event-loop-is-the-lock); one residual (S7 concurrent-write last-writer-wins) accepted as known limitation. Rishabh chose Option B (rebase framing) which collapsed to the event-loop lock — no explicit queue, no rebase loop needed. Spec locked at ~211 LOC net surface, filed at docs/spec-2026-04-17-aurelius-device-sync-reconcile.md in SproutLab. Handoff §4/§5 are now factually stale; spec §15 documents the delta for Lyra.
decisions: Adopt _syncSetDocMerge architectural wrapper — collapses P1R2-2 and P3R4-4 into one structural fix; Edict III reduces to debounce-vs-immediate (chose immediate)
_syncNormalizeOut deletes undefined rather than coercing to null — preserves absent-vs-present-null invariant so ingress discrimination at P4R5-7 works cleanly
MEANINGFUL_NULL_KEYS scoped single-doc only — per-entry model does not have the per-key decision point that the P4R5-7 bug class targets; tombstone semantics deferred
Month partitioning deferred from Session 1 — P7R2-2 race guard dropped; no month-doc architecture to hang it on
Reconcile isolation via HR-14 sync invariant — no await / no yield between _syncReconcileWriting set and clear; event loop is the lock

Rejected Option A (reconcile lock with explicit listener queue) in favor of Option B (event-loop-is-the-lock) — no queue needed because JS single-threading already isolates sync code from listener callbacks
S7 (concurrent-write last-writer-wins) accepted as known limitation, user-facing mitigation text drafted for future user docs
L529 per-entry update uses inline _syncNormalizeOut rather than separate _syncUpdate wrapper — .update() semantics already merge; one wrapper covers set({merge:true}) correctness
Spec filename convention adopts author-in-filename (spec-YYYY-MM-DD-author-subject.md) following the handoff naming pattern
sessions.handoff: Spec R3-locked and filed. sessions.summary: The Codex RPG Design Session — the single longest and most consequential design session in Codex history. Transformed Codex from a library-themed project tracker into a full personal civilization engine with RPG architecture. Designed 16 entity types across 4 layers, a 6-tier Aura rarity system, 3 Architect paths with 12 hybrid classes, 7 Domain levels, 17 AI companions (The Immortal Order), Mortal Companion dossiers, Ink economy, Entropy antagonist, The Quiet burnout detection, The Sanctum spatial home screen with 11 rooms, daily mini games, seasonal events, the Narrator voice, and a 20-phase implementation plan. Produced a 57-page dissertation PDF (codex-dissertation.pdf) as the canonical seed document. Four design mockup iterations (v1–v4) were built, each improving on the last: v1 established the dark sanctum aesthetic, v2 added particle effects and new screens, v3 integrated real repo data (39 canons, 5 volumes, 31 chapters), v4 addressed Aura visual rendering and text sizing. Key design discovery: Aura must be felt (visual atmosphere) not read (text labels). Key philosophical foundation: The Three Pillars — Nothing Is Wasted, The Map Is Not the Territory, Growth Is Fractal Not Linear.
decisions: Lore has 5 categories: Edicts, Origins, Cautionary Tales, Doctrines, Chronicles — names chosen to match the library/ancient-knowledge theme
Prophecies have progressive revelation — waypoints hidden until progress thresholds reached, creating discovery not backlog anxiety
Prophecies have branching paths with hidden branches that reveal at certain waypoints
Prophecy lifecycle: Foretold → Active → Fulfilled/Defied/Suspended — Defied auto-generates Cautionary Tale, Fulfilled auto-generates Origin
Scrolls have 3 origin types (Authored, Acquired, Revealed) and 3 mastery levels (Studied, Practiced, Internalized)
Aura system: Iron → Bronze → Silver → Gold → Amethyst → Obsidian with hard distribution caps (unlimited, 30%, 20%, 10%, 5%, 2%)
Aura is proposed by Codex (computed from Bond count, lineage depth, domain spread) and confirmed by Architect
Aura is visual atmosphere (gradients, shadows, glows, accent lines), NEVER text labels
3 Architect paths: Maker (Craft), Sage (Insight), Sovereign (Influence) — forking at Level 5
12 Hybrid classes emerge at Level 15 from primary path × secondary stat intersection
Transcendent class auto-generated at Level 25 from unique combination of path + hybrid + domain + prophecy + seal data
17 Companions in the Immortal Order: 4 Builders, 3 Strategists, 4 Seekers, 3 Guardians, 3 Wildcards
Companion ranks: Stranger → Acquaintance → Ally → Confidant → Bonded → Soulbound
Mortal Companions: deep dossiers of real people with communication style, decision speed, motivation drivers, friction points, autonomy level
Ink is spendable currency earned proportionally to XP — spent on Aura petitions, Companion gifts, Sanctum customization, Prophecy acceleration, Seal hints
Entropy is the silent antagonist: Scroll Decay, Domain Rust, Companion Drift, Canon Erosion, Prophecy Fog, Lore Sedimentation
The Quiet: burnout detection that halves XP requirements, pauses side quests, dims the Sanctum, and waits patiently — never punishes
The Sanctum: 11 rooms unlocked progressively — Scriptorium, Stacks, Chronicle Hall, Observatory, Forge, Ember Garden, War Table, Companion Quarters, Vault, Atlas Chamber, Inner Sanctum
Dawn Page is the daily arrival — Whisper, Rite candles, Active Fires, Companion greeting, one mini game. Dusk Reflection is the evening counterpart.
The Binding: annual narrative auto-compiled from the year's Prophecies, Relics, Lore, Rites, Echoes — reads like an autobiography chapter
Side Quests: 7 types — Bounties, Errands, Challenges, Mysteries, Pilgrimages, Diplomatic Missions, Scroll Expeditions
8 Daily Mini Games: Whisper Response, The Forge, Archive Roulette, Pip's Riddle, Domain Duel, Cartographer's Game, Echo Forecast, Companion's Question
4 Seasonal Events: Great Cataloguing (quarterly), The Reckoning (epoch transition), Ember Storm (luminary trigger), Prophecy Convergence (rare)
The Three Pillars established as philosophical foundation: Nothing Is Wasted, The Map Is Not the Territory, Growth Is Fractal Not Linear
Minimum text size: 9px floor across entire app — daily-use demands eye comfort
Sound is opt-in by default, with per-room ambiance, per-event sounds, and per-companion audio signatures
20-phase implementation plan estimated at 50–70 sessions (4–6 months)

Design language needs 3–4 more iteration rounds before production-ready (v4 is functional but unpolished)

sessions.summary: SEP Invoicing Phase 8A: Desktop Layout Foundation. Added breakpoint detection (_isDesktop/_isTablet/_pendingModeSwitch), icon sidebar with hover-expand and touch fallback, debounced ResizeObserver, deferred mode switch (overlay/form-safe), desktop overlay as centered modal, tablet wider cards. 6 files modified, +215 lines (6041 to 6256). 6 QA rounds caught 5 bugs before deploy.
decisions: ResizeObserver over matchMedia — catches all resize triggers including browser chrome, split-screen, DevTools
Deferred mode switch — _pendingModeSwitch flag prevents DOM destruction mid-overlay/form interaction
Touch sidebar fallback — tap-to-expand on non-hover devices instead of CSS :hover
Tablet = big phone — bottom tabs stay, wider cards, no max-width cap
Separate rendering strategy — desktop will use dedicated render functions (8C-8F), not CSS-only responsive
sessions.handoff: Phase 8A deployed. Foundation is live: sidebar navigation, breakpoint detection, overlay split, tablet overrides. Next: Phase 8B (master-detail containers + drag handle + persistence).
